allies.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory gives scaffolding its operating logic by explaining why chunking and staged support reduce overload during learning.
allies.source_quote: Scaffolding is the implementation mechanism for respecting the limitations described by Cognitive Load Theory.

allies.rationale_text: Boundaries help scaffolding define what belongs inside the current learning frame so attention stays on the next useful increment.
allies.source_quote: Scaffolding provides an essential structure that establishes boundaries, helping decision-makers focus on what is inside the frame.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Scaffolding conflicts with desirable-difficulties when load reduction removes the productive challenge needed for durable learning transfer.
structured_tensions.source_quote: scaffolding vs desirable-difficulties: scaffolding conflicts with desirable-difficulties (desirable difficulties) when desirable Difficulties emphasizes challenging learning conditions, contrasting with Scaffolding's focus on reducing cognitive load.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Scaffolding conflicts with confirmation-bias when the support design reflects the teacher's assumptions instead of diagnosed learner needs.
structured_tensions.source_quote: scaffolding vs confirmation-bias: scaffolding conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when scaffold design reflects the instructor's beliefs about what learners need rather than evidence-based diagnosis of actual knowledge gaps.

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on CLT-backed support design and an explicit tension block around over-smoothing and diagnostic drift.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader pedagogy relation sprawl beyond the cleanest current-vocabulary support and tension links.
open_questions: Blooms-taxonomy is source-real as an ally, but the first-order surface is cleaner around cognitive-load-theory and boundaries.
